channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Strands #774 Hints and Answers for Today, April 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #774 for Thursday, April 16, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters and under do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, April 16, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #774 are all related to terms you use to describe your hobbies

General Strands Hints For April 16

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: The “rec” in rec room.
  • Hint 2: Baseball, the great American ____.
  • Hint 3: When you have interests or skills that you do for leisure.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #774 for April 16, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for April 16, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • FORE
  • TASER
  • JUST
  • PAST
  • TIME

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #774 On April 16, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • PASTIME
  • INTEREST
  • RECREATION
  • HOBBY
  • SIDELINE
  • SPANAGRAM: JUSTFORFUN

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #774?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on April 17 will be Strands Game #775.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett serves as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since its launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-strands-774-hints-and-answers-for-today-april-16-2026/

item.title: Epic Games’ Chief People Officer Gone From Company
item.content:encoded:
Monika Fahlbusch has parted ways with Epic Games. Labeled the “Chief People Officer,” Fahlbusch was with the company for just over 5 years, starting in December 2020. According to a new report, Epic has confirmed her departure.

As a top executive in the HR department, Fahlbusch reportedly played a role in the latest round of cuts, which saw the Fortnite publisher lay off more than 1,000 people in the latter half of March 2026. In the wake of the cuts, Fahlbusch sent a letter to the North Carolina Department of Commerce where she stated on behalf of the company, “We are mindful of the difficulties that this layoff poses to employees and the community.”

Epic Games Confirms Departure of HR Exec Monika Fahlbusch

According to a Kotaku report, Epic Games confirmed that Monika Fahlbusch’s last day with the company was April 15. However, the company ultimately declined to elaborate on the reason she was exiting her role.

Her previous work experience before joining Epic included two years in a similar role for JUUL Labs. Experience notes that she has over 30 years of experience leading “People functions.” Her additional experience includes roles at Salesforce and BMC Software.

More on the Recent Epic Layoffs

When Epic Games announced it was laying off more than 1,000 employees in March 2026, the difficult decision was blamed on lower-than-expected engagement in Fortnite and other areas of the games business. Unfortunately, one of the individuals laid off was programmer-writer Mike Prinke, an individual battling terminal brain cancer.

Following the cuts, his wife shared on social media that they were concerned about him losing the life insurance he received as an employee benefit at Epic. In the wake of that, Epic CEO Tim Sweeney issued a statement apologizing for not recognizing the situation in advance and claimed that Prinke’s medical issue was not a factor in the decision to lay him off. Learn more about that at this link.

Insider Gaming will continue to monitor this story and report any updates.

What are your thoughts on the departure of this Epic Games HR executive? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/epics-head-of-hr-gone-from-company/

item.title: Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred DLC Rated for Nintendo Switch in Indonesia
item.content:encoded:
Diablo 4’s Lord of Hatred DLC has apparently been rated by Indonesia’s ratings board for a new console. Recently, the API for the Indonesia Game Rating System (IGRS) revealed a rating for the Diablo 4 Lord of Hatred DLC specifically for the Nintendo Switch.

Specifically, the content is rated for the original Nintendo Switch, as well as for PC, Sony PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series consoles.

The IGRS’ Rating for Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred Explained

The Indonesia Game Ratings System rated the forthcoming Diablo 4 expansion 18+. 18+ is the second-most mature rating the board gives. The next rating underneath it is RC (refused classification). That rating is reserved for games that contain prohibited content akin to what would land a game with an AO rating in the ESRB.

The full descriptor for the 18+ rating can be seen below:

“Game content suitable for ages 18 and over (18+) does not contain pornography, but may display references to cigarettes, alcohol, or drugs; animated violence, blood, mutilation, and cannibalism; adult humor with sexual connotations; human-like characters without showing genitals, breasts, or buttocks; gambling activities that do not involve real or digital currency; horror elements intended to provoke fear; and online conversation features.”

More on Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred

The major expansion for Diablo 4, coming on April 28, is slated to introduce two new classes for players to use. One of those classes is the Paladin, but that is exclusive to players who pre-order the expansion. It was also previously announced that the update would overhaul skill trees, with the development team joking back in February that they were putting an end to players calling the existing skill tree system “skill twigs.”

Every class will receive skill tree updates, including over 40 choices and more than 80 additional options. Players who purchased the Lord of Hatred DLC will also have 20 extra transformative skill options.

Learn more about the upcoming expansion here.

What do you think about the possibility of Diablo 4’s latest expansion coming to the Nintendo Switch family of consoles? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Union Wins Settlement for Ubisoft Halifax Laid-Off Staff
item.content:encoded:
The laid-off workers from Ubisoft’s Halifax branch have voted in favor of a settlement with the company.

In January, it was reported that the CWA Canada union, representing 61 of the 71 employees who lost their jobs when Ubisoft Halifax was shut down, had filed a legal complaint. The studio closed days after the announcement that 61 of its employees had joined the Game & Media Workers Guild of Canada, Local 30111.

CWA Canada Secures Deal for Ubisoft Halifax Workers

In a new announcement, the union revealed that its members voted overwhelmingly in favor of the settlement with Ubisoft.

The union negotiated the deal for its members. There is also a statement from a former Ubisoft employee expressing gratitude to the union for negotiating the package for the impacted staffers. There’s also a statement from the union’s president.

“The success of the negotiation was directly attributable to the solidarity our members showed, their resolve in holding Ubisoft to account, the collecting and sharing of information, and remaining steadfast in advocating for their rights as unionized workers,” CWA Canada President Carmel Smyth said. “Our union is very proud to have provided these workers with the help of our staff and lawyers to work through this difficult time.”

The CWA Canada President also noted that Ubisoft respected the union and acted professionally, but that negotiations weren’t easy.

For more on Ubisoft’s recent financial struggles, check out the canceled projects that resulted from these closures.

Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest news on all things gaming.

How do you feel about this announcement? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: When Will Pokémon Champions Add More Pokémon?
item.content:encoded:
Pokémon Champions released with a limited number of Pokémon and items available to make it more beginner-friendly.

Many players have been learning how to play VGC for the first time now that there is no need to grind for IVs, EVs etc.

The limited roster has led to a fresh meta, which has also been refreshing for some VGC veterans with older picks returning to the forefront.

However, that doesn’t mean that the player base isn’t eagerly waiting for the playable roster to expand. Especially with a lot more Mega Evolutions from Pokémon Legends Z-A that have never been playable competitively and haven’t had their abilities revealed yet.

Currently, Pokémon Champions has 187 Pokémon playable in the game, including 59 Mega Evolutions, but that could change in a couple of months.

Pokémon Champions is expected to undergo large updates, which will change the roster and rules available in ranked battles.

Especially as the characters have Omni Rings, which allow the use of any battle gimmick such as Z-Moves, Dynamax, and Terastallization.

When Will The Pokémon Champions Roster Be Expanded?

The Pokémon Company hasn’t confirmed when the Pokémon Champion roster will change.

Yet, we have a pretty good idea of when there may be any rotations or expansions to the roster of playable Pokémon.

By the schedule revealed on the Pokémon Champions website, Regulation Set M-B will release with Season M-3.

The game is currently in Regulation Set M-A and Season M-1, with the current ranked season ending on May 12.

Season 2 could add some more Pokémon to the game, though it has already had its battle pass rewards leak and it only included Pokémon already in the game.

However, the start of Regulation Set M-B and Season M-3, which should start in June judging by this season’s length, is when we will likely see big changes to the game.

Especially as on the recruit screen in Pokémon Champions, it says that the current roster is available until the end of June 16.

This could also coincide with the leaked mobile release window of Pokémon Champions.

The official Pokémon Champions website also backs up that this is when we will see roster changes, stating:

“New regulations for Ranked Battles will be set every few seasons to mix up the competition and keep Trainers on their toes.

“The Pokémon you can use and other parameters will change with each new set of regulations. You never know—new Pokémon just might become eligible.”

What Pokémon and items do you want to see added to Pokémon Champions? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And prepare for more additions by learning how to expand your Pokémon Champions team slots for free.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/when-will-pokemon-champions-add-more-pokemon/

item.title: All You Need To Know About TFT at the Esports World Cup 2026
item.content:encoded:
Teamfight Tactics (TFT) is returning at this year’s Esports World Cup, taking place from July 21 to July 25 during Week 3 of the event.

The auto-battler by Riot Games will feature at the Saudi Arabia-based event for the third consecutive year.

Last year’s event was won by Weibo Gaming, who went undefeated throughout the entire tournament and defeated giant killers Virtus.Pro 3-1 in the final.

This year’s TFT EWC tournament will feature a $500k prize pool and will see the return of its unique team-based format.

TFT Esports World Cup Prize Pool

The prize breakdown for this year’s TFT tournament has not been revealed as of yet.

However, considering it features the same $500k prize pool as last year, the 16-team tournament will likely feature the same distributions we saw last year:

  • 1st — $150k
  • 2nd — $70k
  • 3rd-4th — $40k
  • 5-8th — $25k
  • 9th-12th — $15k
  • 13th-16th — $10k

There will also be more money available this year with an increased prize pool of $30m for the Club Championship.

This initiative financially rewards the esports organisations that perform best across multiple titles at this year’s Esports World Cup.

TFT Esports World Cup Format

While TFT is usually a game played solo, the Esports World Cup turns the auto-battler into a roster-based esport with teams of four competing against each other.

This year, the format will be as follows:

  • Group Stage: August 11 – 13
    • 16 teams are split into two even groups of eight teams.
    • For each group, eight teams compete in a Double-Elimination (GSL) format.
      • All matches are best-of-three.
      • Top four teams from each group advance to the Playoffs.
  • Playoffs: August 14 – 15
    • Eight teams qualified from the Group Stage compete in the Single-Elimination format.
      • All matches (excl. Grand Final) are best-of-three.
        • Grand Final is best-of-five.

Who Will Compete At The TFT Esports World Cup?

So far, only one TFT team has qualified for this year’s Esports World Cup, and that’s reigning champions Weibo Gaming.

The champions receive an automatic invitation and will be keeping an eye on the qualifiers to see who they will defend the title against.

There will be 15 other teams that qualify for the event, with Qualifier tournaments being hosted all around the world.

The following Qualifiers will directly decide the other TFT competitors:

  • The top three TFT teams from Road To EWC 26: Online Qualifier – Americas
  • The top three TFT teams from Road To EWC 26: Online Qualifier – EMEA
  • The top three TFT teams from Road To EWC 26: Online Qualifier – APAC
  • The top three TFT teams from Road To EWC 26: Online Qualifier – China
  • The top three TFT teams from Road To EWC 26: Online Qualifier – Global Qualifier

Do you like team-based TFT or do you prefer watching it as a solo esport? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see what teams were selected as Esports World Cup 2026 partner clubs with FaZe Clan and Karmine Corp amongst a few surprising omissions.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/all-you-need-to-know-about-tft-at-the-esports-world-cup-2026/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Alan Wake is Coming to Phasmophobia Next Month
item.content:encoded:
Phasmophobia’s first collaboration has been confirmed: Alan Wake is coming to the game as a free event in May 2026.

At the beginning of this year, Kinetic Games unveiled the 2026 roadmap for Phasmophobia. This roadmap included comprehensive details about major events, new maps, and various updates. However, it did not reveal any information about potential collaborations. This lack of detail makes sense, as they were planning something special with Remedy Games.

The Worlds of Alan Wake and Phasmophobia Collide This May

During the Galaxies Showcase event, Kinetic Games announced the first-ever collaboration for Phasmophobia, and it’s with none other than Alan Wake. The crossover will come through a new event called ‘Phasmophobia by Alan Wake’, which features “shifting overlap between worlds, bringing the haunting atmosphere of Alan Wake 2 into Phasmophobia.” The upcoming collaboration will be available in the game from May 12, 2026.

The announcement comes right after Phasmophobia hit a new milestone, crossing 5 million copies sold on consoles and 28 million overall. Check out what Sam Lake, the Creative Director at Remedy, had to say about the crossover:

“There are many fans of Phasmophobia at Remedy, so it was a thrilling opportunity to bring these two horror worlds together in this dream – or rather, nightmare – collaboration. It was a joy to write this. Our two twisted worlds amplify each other perfectly.”

Daniel Knight, the CEO of Kinetic Games, also shared his thoughts on the new collab:

“Working with Remedy has been an incredible experience, and the way these two worlds overlap has opened up some unsettling possibilities that we’re excited for players to explore. It’s a natural crossover, and our art team has been hard at work capturing the atmosphere of Alan Wake 2, bringing the essence of some of the game’s iconic locations into Phasmophobia. We can’t wait for fans of both games to see what the event has in store on May 12th.”

In other news, Remedy has confirmed that Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 will receive support for PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR. What are your thoughts on Phasmophobia x Alan Wake? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced appearing on Ubisoft’s launcher. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
